key_insight: A hybrid GNN-LSTM surrogate can learn the static mechanical response of additively manufactured short-fiber thermoplastics directly from microstructure topology and loading history.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, physics-informed screening of weak microstructural regions and makes digital-twin workflows for SFT parts far more practical than full mesoscale FE analysis.
method: Reconstruct microstructures from micro-CT and discretize them into Voronoi-based fiber-interaction cells.
Homogenize each cell with nonlinear FE simulations that include matrix damage.
Train a GNN-LSTM to map microstructural graph structure and strain history to stress-strain response.
evidence_signal: The surrogate matches unseen FE responses with about R^2 ≈ 0.98 while reducing computational cost by more than two orders of magnitude.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of micro-CT reconstruction, calibration of damage laws, and how well training data cover the variability of real manufactured microstructures.
paper_title: On Surrogate Modeling of Static Response of AM Short-Fiber Thermoplastics Using Graph Neural Networks
